name: Maya
education: Sophomore at Fresno State, majoring in Communications. First in my family to go to a four-year university.
work-experience: Part-time barista at a local cafe for two years, and a volunteer peer tutor in the campus writing center.
easy-tasks: Making nervous people feel comfortable and explaining confusing things in plain language.
energizing-tasks: Helping a classmate finally understand something, or organizing a small group around a shared goal.
complimented-skills: People always tell me I am a really good listener and that I make them feel heard.
natural-talents: They would say I am patient, warm, and good at bringing people together.
focusing-tasks: Tutoring writing and editing other people’s essays — I look up and hours have passed.
fulfilling-work: Running a study group for first-gen students last semester felt completely natural and fulfilling.
world-problem: I would make sure every first-generation student had someone in their corner who believed in them.
industries: Education, community nonprofits, and public media inspire me.
companies: Mission-driven nonprofits and community organizations that put people before profit.
people: My mom, my high school counselor, and Mister Rogers — people who lead with warmth.
conversation: Talking about what makes people tick and how to help communities support each other.
time: I would mentor students, volunteer at the library, and tell community stories.
energizing: A warm, collaborative setting where I work closely with a small, supportive team.
flexibility: I like a good amount of flexibility, but I appreciate having a clear rhythm to my week.
work-culture: A culture that is kind, inclusive, and genuinely cares about people over numbers.
work-setting: A welcoming community space or a friendly open office, not an isolated cubicle.
scheduling: Mostly steady daytime hours with room to adjust when life happens.
stress: A gentle, manageable level of pressure — enough to focus me, not so much that I burn out.
climate: A mild, sunny climate like California’s Central Valley suits me.
scene: A friendly, community-minded town with local cafes and gathering spots.
proximity: I’d like to stay fairly close to home and my family.
transport: Walking and the occasional bus, with a car for longer trips.
nature: Being near parks and a bit of nature matters a lot to my well-being.
values-selection: Generosity – Giving freely to help others
Balance – Maintaining stability across life areas
values-selection-2: Generosity – Giving freely to help others
job-sector: Education or community nonprofit work, ideally student support or outreach.
anything-else: I want work that feels meaningful and lets me help people who are often overlooked.
dream-job-requirements: Yes, I am ready to see what it takes.
question-1: Processing my feelings and/or seeking support
question-7: Keep options open and adjust as needed
question-10: Explore freely and learn through experience
question-11: Trust my instincts and adapt as I go
question-13: Aim for more abstract, exciting possibilities
question-22: Trust my gut feeling about what's right
question-24: Allow the process to evolve naturally based on team input
question-25: Take quick action based on my instinct/gut feeling
question-30: Stay flexible and adapt as needed
question-32: Focus on big ideas and possibilities
question-35: Find one person for a deep conversation
